db.db_description: This database is an instance of the OHDSI OMOP Common Data Model (CDM), a standardized schema for observational health research, populated with Synthea-generated synthetic patient data. It comprises three coordinated layers: (1) a standardized vocabulary layer (concept, concept_relationship, concept_ancestor, concept_synonym, vocabulary, domain, relationship, drug_strength, and lookups like concept_class) that defines and inter-relates clinical concepts drawn from terminologies such as SNOMED, RxNorm, ICD, and UCUM; (2) a clinical data layer centered on the person table (2,694 synthetic patients with demographics) and their longitudinal events — visits (visit_occurrence), diagnoses (condition_occurrence and aggregated condition_era), drugs (drug_exposure and aggregated drug_era), procedures (procedure_occurrence), measurements, and observations — all mapped to standardized concepts and tied to persons and encounters; and (3) supporting health-system, economic, and metadata structures (provider, care_site, location, cost, payer_plan_period, death, and CDM administrative tables like cdm_source and metadata). Many tables — including economic, device, specimen, note/NLP, and detailed visit tables — are present but empty, reflecting the limited scope of the synthetic source. The database's purpose is to enable standardized analytics and cohort studies over patient-level clinical histories.

concept.reasoning: This is the OMOP CDM standardized vocabulary CONCEPT table. Column names (concept_id, concept_name, domain_id, vocabulary_id, concept_class_id, standard_concept, concept_code, valid_start_date, valid_end_date, invalid_reason) exactly match the OMOP Common Data Model CONCEPT table. Sample values confirm: domain_id holds Drug/Condition/Measurement; vocabulary_id holds RxNorm/SNOMED/LOINC/CVX; standard_concept = 'S'; concept_code source codes like NDC, LOINC (1751-7). concept_id is unique PK.

columns.name: vocabulary_id
columns.description: Code identifying the source vocabulary the concept comes from (e.g. RxNorm, SNOMED, LOINC, CVX, Visit).

columns.name: concept_class_id
columns.description: Code/enum for the class of the concept within its vocabulary (e.g. Clinical Finding, Ingredient, Clinical Drug, Procedure, Lab Test, 11-digit NDC).

columns.name: standard_concept
columns.description: Flag marking whether the concept is a standard concept; observed value 'S' (standard), null when not standard/classification. Low cardinality enum.

columns.name: concept_code
columns.description: The original source code for the concept within its vocabulary (e.g. NDC number, LOINC code 1751-7, RxNorm code); unique per row here.

columns.name: valid_start_date

columns.name: valid_end_date
columns.description: Date until which the concept is valid; 2099-12-31 indicates currently valid (no end).

columns.name: invalid_reason

concept_ancestor.reasoning: Table name and column names match the OMOP CDM CONCEPT_ANCESTOR table exactly. The neighbour CONCEPT table is the standardized vocabulary. Both ancestor_concept_id and descendant_concept_id reference concepts (descendant FK confirmed to concept.concept_id; ancestor is also a concept_id). min/max levels of separation are small integers (0-14) representing hierarchy distance. This is a precomputed transitive closure of the concept hierarchy.
concept_ancestor.table_description: The OMOP CDM CONCEPT_ANCESTOR table: each row records a hierarchical ancestor-descendant relationship between two standardized vocabulary concepts, with the minimum and maximum number of hierarchy levels separating them. It is a precomputed transitive closure of the concept hierarchy used to roll up or drill down concepts.

columns.name: ancestor_concept_id
columns.description: Concept ID of the ancestor (more general/higher-level) concept in the hierarchy; references a concept in the CONCEPT table.

columns.name: descendant_concept_id
columns.description: Concept ID of the descendant (more specific/lower-level) concept in the hierarchy; foreign key to concept.concept_id.

columns.name: min_levels_of_separation
columns.description: Minimum number of hierarchy levels (steps) between the ancestor and descendant concepts; 0 indicates the concept is its own ancestor (self-relationship).

columns.name: max_levels_of_separation
columns.description: Maximum number of hierarchy levels (steps) between the ancestor and descendant concepts along the longest path.
